data.title: MeiraGTx: Turning Dry Mouth Into Liquid Gold
data.description:
MeiraGTx Overview

With MeiraGTx’s (MGTX) stock up 50% in the past month, I wanted to see what the "fuss" is all about. Although this is a name I am familiar with, I have yet to do a formal evaluation.

MeiraGTx describes themselves as a “clinical-stage genetic medicine company committed to harnessing the full potential of genetic therapies for both rare and prevalent diseases.”

MeiraGTx does things a bit differently than, say, other gene therapy companies, like CRISPR Therapeutics (CRSP). MeiraGTx uses riboswitch gene regulation to allow for precise, dose-responsive control of gene expression using an oral small-molecule inducer.

MeiraGTx is also unique in that it is “vertically integrated.” That is, it does not rely on contract manufacturing organizations (like many of its peers in biotech).

Its pipeline is ambitious—targeting both rare (i.e., ALS) and common diseases (i.e., radiation-induced xerostomia, Parkinson's).

Ahead of Phase 1 data for AAV-hAQP1 for radiation-induced xerostomia (expected on April 16), MeiraGTx’s stock has reached 52-week highs, now valued at nearly $1B (market capitalization).

Below, I take a look at the market opportunity in radiation-induced xerostomia, as well as other upcoming catalysts.

Radiation-Induced Xerostomia

The market for radiation-induced xerostomia is a bit tricky, which probably explains why the company went out of its way to say it will also “include a section on the drug’s commercial opportunity.”

The Phase 1 study is an open-label, non-randomized, dose escalation trial that enrolled 24 patients to primarily evaluate for AAV-hAQP1’s safety.

It delivers a single dose of AAV-hAQP1 into one or both parotid glands and observes the patients over years. AAV-hAQP1 has since advanced into a Phase 2 trial.

MeiraGTx revealed positive Phase 1 data in December 2022, which I have summarized in the table below.

Following the data, AAV-hAQP1 was awarded with RMAT and Breakthrough Therapy Designation. The latter was specifically supported by the 3-year data that will be fully unveiled to the public on Thursday.

Now, radiation-induced xerostomia is a common and debilitating side effect of radiation for head and neck cancers. The company is specifically targeting survivors who suffer from persistent Grade 2/3 radiation-induced xerostomia (fibrotic and non-functional). There is currently no approved curative treatment.

Although there are 170k survivors (estimate) with chronic Grade 2/3 radiation-induced xerostomia, as the company cites, I’d be wary of using this as a Target Pop. when modeling AAV-hAQP1.

In its Phase 1 and Phase 2 clinical trials, MeiraGTx enrolls “long-term” survivors and “mid-term” survivors, respectively.

There are roughly 70k new cases of HNC annually in the US, and about 30% will develop permanent, clinically significant xerostomia despite modern sparing techniques like IMRT. This gives AAV-hAQP1 a “new incident flow” of 21k. I also accounted for 1.5% annual Pop. Growth.

Again, TAM is up for interpretation, but I like to lean conservative and realistic.

To balance my conservatism, I also assume a 20% Yr 1 Uptake (which is very aggressive) and a 25% Peak Share. I set Probability of Success, or POS, at 45%, which is higher than the typical Phase 2 therapy. POS could increase further depending on how the data looks tomorrow. I have Launch Year at 2028, Peak Year at 2034, and LOE at 2040 (12-year biologic exclusivity). Net Price estimates could vary, but $200k leans conservative for a one-time “functional cure.” And, although xerostomia is debilitating, it isn’t "life or death." COGS is at 8%. Launch OpEx is 45% for 3 Yrs. Steady-State OpEx (after 3 Yrs of Launch) drops to 25%. I use a WACC discount rate of 12% to account for clinical-stage risk.

I have AAV-hAQP1 generating roughly $1.2B at peak, and worth $979.8M in risk-adjusted Net Present Value. And should further data increase POS to, say, 55%, its rNPV could feasibly jump over $1.2B.

Broader Pipeline Implications

AAV-hAQP1 is also being evaluated in Sjögren’s Syndrome, which is a much larger autoimmune indication that impacts as many as 4 million Americans. So, not only could tomorrow’s data de-risk radiation-induced xerostomia, but it could also help validate larger targets.

Like radiation-induced xerostomia, Sjögren’s is also a “water channel” problem. They share pathology (destroyed acinar cells). AAV-hAQP1 works because it inserts the hAQP1 gene, which creates a “pore” that allows water to move passively through these ductal cells.

MGTX Stock Financial Health

MeiraGTx ended the year with $65.9M in cash and investments.

While the company makes money from big pharma collaborations like Eli Lilly (LLY) and Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), it is not enough to offset R&D and SG&A expenses. In fact, the company “burned” (cash from operations) $46.4M in 2025, giving them ~6 quarters of cash runway. MeiraGTx expects its cash runway to extend "into the second half of 2027."

In early 2026, MeiraGTx received an additional $55M from the Hologen collaboration in Parkinson’s. In March, there was an amendment of the Perceptive credit facility, which extended its debt to May 2027 ($25M due June 2026, $50M due May 2027).

So, although lumpy, the company does have some financial flexibility.

Still, I think there’s a good chance of an equity raise in the near future.

Conclusion

Tomorrow’s update is kind of a big deal for AAV-hAQP1 and MeiraGTx’s broader platform. Positive data (which seems quite likely, judging from Breakthrough Therapy Designation) could not only de-risk the potential ~$1.2B peak-sales xerostomia opportunity, but also help validate the same water-channel mechanism in the much larger Sjögren’s indication.

With vertically-integrated riboswitch-controlled tech and big pharma partnerships, I think MGTX, despite its recent run-up, offers significant upside (initiate with a "Buy" rating) for investors who can tolerate clinical-stage biotech volatility and risks (i.e., dilution, clinical trial failure, regulatory setbacks, etc.).
